2605.15179.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
2605.15179.paper_summary: Scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) toward universal foundation models is bottlenecked by negative transfer: the simultaneous co-training of disparate partial differential equation (PDE) regimes can induce gradient conflict, unstable optimization, and plasticity loss in dense neural operators. In particular, broadband open-channel fluid dynamics and boundary-dominated porous media flows impose incompatible spectral and geometric demands on a single dense parameter path. We introduce Shodh-MoE, a sparse-activated latent transformer architecture for multi-physics transport. Shodh-MoE operates on compressed 16^3 physical latents produced by a physics-informed autoencoder with an intra-tokenizer Helmholtz-style velocity parameterization, restricting decoded states to divergence-free velocity manifolds. The model guarantees exact mass conservation, achieving a physically verifiable velocity divergence of ~2.8 x 10^-10 (evaluated post-hoc in FP64) on 128^3 grids. A Top-1 soft-semantic router dynamically assigns localized latent patches to expert subnetworks, enabling specialized parameter paths for distinct physical mechanisms while preserving shared experts for universal symmetries. In a 20,000-step distributed pretraining run over mixed three-dimensional physical tensors, routing telemetry shows autonomous domain bifurcation: held-out validation tokens from the open-channel domain route exclusively to Expert 0, while porous-media tokens route exclusively to Expert 1. The model converges simultaneously across both regimes, achieving latent validation MSEs of 2.46 x 10^-5 and 9.76 x 10^-6, and decoded physical MSEs of 2.48 x 10^-6 and 1.76 x 10^-6. These results support sparse expert routing as a practical architectural mechanism for mitigating multi-physics interference in universal neural operators.
2605.15179.paper_summary_zh: 擴展科學機器學習（SciML）至通用基礎模型受到負轉移的瓶頸：不同偏微分方程（PDE）範疇的同時共同訓練可能會引發梯度衝突、不穩定的優化和密集神經運算元的可塑性損失。特別是，寬頻開放通道流體動力學和邊界主導的多孔介質流對單一密集參數路徑施加了不相容的光譜和幾何需求。我們介紹了Shodh-MoE，一種稀疏激活的潛在Transformer架構，用於多物理傳輸。Shodh-MoE在由物理知識自編碼器生成的壓縮16^3物理潛變量上運行，並使用內部標記器的亥姆霍茲風速參數化，限制解碼狀態為無散度的速度流形。該模型保證精確的質量守恆，在128^3網格上實現了約2.8 x 10^-10的物理可驗證速度散度（在FP64中事後評估）。一個Top-1軟語義路由器動態地將局部潛在區塊分配給專家子網絡，使得不同物理機制的專門參數路徑得以實現，同時保留共享專家以適應通用對稱性。在一個20,000步的分佈式預訓練運行中，涉及混合三維物理張量，路由遙測顯示自動域分岔：來自開放通道域的保留驗證標記專門路由至專家0，而多孔介質標記則專門路由至專家1。該模型在兩個範疇中同時收斂，實現了潛在驗證均方誤差（MSE）為2.46 x 10^-5和9.76 x 10^-6，以及解碼的物理均方誤差為2.48 x 10^-6和1.76 x 10^-6。這些結果支持稀疏專家路由作為減輕通用神經運算元中多物理干擾的實用架構機制。
2605.15179.author: Ellwil Sharma et.al.
